subject: Product Management
title: How Apple Prioritizes
description: A quick win: understand How Apple Prioritizes.

hook.text: Apple's product lineup is remarkably small. While competitors offer dozens of models, Apple focuses on a handful done perfectly.

buildup.text: When Steve Jobs returned in 1997, he drew a 2×2 grid: consumer/pro and desktop/portable. Four products. Everything else was cut.

discovery.text: Apple's prioritization philosophy: build fewer things but make each one insanely great. Depth over breadth.

twist.text: This constraint is culturally enforced. Teams compete for the limited roadmap slots. Only the strongest ideas survive.

climax.text: Apple proves that extreme focus is a competitive advantage. Fewer products means more resources per product.

punchline.text: Do fewer things. Do each one insanely well. That's Apple's secret.

quiz.question: What is Apple's approach to product prioritization?
options: Build fewer products but make each one exceptional — depth over breadth
Build as many products as possible to cover every market
Let each team decide independently what to build
